visual_description: A man with short dark hair and a visible neck tattoo is seated inside a vehicle, his head turned slightly to his right as he looks toward another person. The second individual, partially visible and wearing a dark shirt, leans in close, their hand resting near the first man’s neck. The scene is dimly lit, with the interior of the car and blurred background foliage visible through the windows.
audio_description: The audio clip begins with a sharp, forceful inhalation, immediately followed by a male voice speaking in Mandarin Chinese. The speaker, whose accent is standard Putonghua and whose age is estimated to be between 30 and 50, delivers the phrase, “撞了车，很多警察会来” (“I crashed the car, many police will come”). His speech is slow, deliberate, and marked by a strained, raspy quality, with each word separated by heavy, audible breaths. The voice is low-pitched and, as if the speaker is physically and mentally drained. The recording environment is acoustically dry, with no reverberation or background noise, indicating a small, enclosed space such as a car interior. The microphone is positioned close to the speaker, capturing every detail of his labored breathing and vocal strain, while the audio itself is of moderate fidelity, with a persistent low-level hiss and a faint hum, likely from the recording device or nearby electronics. After the speaker finishes, there is a brief pause filled only by his continued heavy breathing, which remains prominent and unaccompanied by any other sounds. The tone is one of resignation and fatigue, with no signs of panic
